articles.title: DRIVERS DIE IN BUS AND TRUCK SMASH: Police warn rogue motorists
articles.description: B-Metro Reporter A DEADLY smash on the Harare–Masvingo Road has left two drivers dead as the Zimbabwe Republic Police urges the public to report reckless motorists before more lives are...The post DRIVERS DIE IN BUS AND TRUCK SMASH: Police warn rogue motorists appeared first on herald.

articles.title: Rising anger over ‘lop-sided’ and ‘immoral’ US health funding pacts with African countries
articles.description: Zimbabwe refuses to sign agreement and Kenya faces a court case over data sharing as new aid deals come under scrutiny

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Prominent lawyer Madhuku beaten by ZANU-PF terrorists in balaclavas ‘as police watch’
more.description: HARARE – Prominent lawyer and opposition leader Professor Lovemore Madhuku was brutally beaten on Sunday after balaclava-wearing men stormed a meeting of his National Constitutional Assembly (NCA) party in Harare, with uniformed police officers looking on from two nearby vehicles without intervening. Madhuku, who is currently receiving treatment at an undisclosed hospital in the capital, [...]The post Prominent lawyer Madhuku beaten by ZANU-PF terrorists in balaclavas ‘as police watch’ appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Permanent secretary sits on board of company regulated by his ministry
more.description: HARARE – The permanent secretary in the ministry of industry and trade has retained his position on the board of a private company regulated by his ministry, raising serious conflict-of-interest concerns and questions over compliance with public service regulations. Thomas Utete Wushe, who was appointed permanent secretary on October 6, 2023, is the board chairperson [...]The post Permanent secretary sits on board of company regulated by his ministry appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Zanu PF Matabeleland South PCC meets in Gwanda over Constitutional Amendment No. 3 Bill of 2026
more.description: Sukulwenkosi Dube-Matutu THE Zanu PF Matabeleland South Provincial Coordinating Committee (PCC) has gathered in Gwanda for a national address by the party’s leadership on the Constitutional Amendment No. 3 Bill of 2026. Government on 16 February gazetted Constitutional Amendment No. 3 Bill of 2026, kickstarting a 90-day public consultation period, which will culminate in Parliament [...]The post Zanu PF Matabeleland South PCC meets in Gwanda over Constitutional Amendment No. 3 Bill of 2026 appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Government gazettes new parking rates, abolishes cattle levy
more.description: Nqobile Bhebhe Government has gazetted new parking rates capped at US$0,50 per hour, US$20 clamping fees and abolished the cattle levy as part of sweeping measures aimed at improving the ease of doing business and reducing the cost burden on motorists and farmers. The new measures are contained in Statutory Instrument 41 of 2026, which [...]The post Government gazettes new parking rates, abolishes cattle levy appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Man granted bail in alleged maize field sexual assault
more.description: Dalyn Chigwizura A 27-year-old Bulawayo man has appeared in court facing rape charges after he allegedly attacked a woman and dragged her into a maize field before sexually assaulting her. Marvelous Tshuma (27) of Mbundane suburb appeared before Western Commonage regional magistrate Mr Pasipanodya Maturure on Thursday charged with rape. He was granted US$200 bail [...]The post Man granted bail in alleged maize field sexual assault appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Two Detectives Shot Dead By An Armed Robber In Zaka
more.description: Two officers from the Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) Criminal Investigations Department (CID) Homicide Division from Harare were shot dead by an armed robber in Zaka, Masvingo, on Sunday morning.

more.title: Unprocedural Constitutional Amendment causes chaos in Zimbabwe as Government cracks down on dissenting voices
more.description: Source: Unprocedural Constitutional Amendment causes chaos in Zimbabwe as Government cracks down on dissenting voices Dear Media, Violence has erupted in Zimbabwe following disgruntlement by citizens over the unprocedural process Government has embarked on to amend the country’s constitution to extend the length of President Emmerson Mnangagwa without a referendum. more.title: Caught red-handed — Government finally explains why it had to ban all raw mineral & lithium exports
more.description: Source: Caught red-handed — Government finally explains why it had to ban all raw mineral & lithium exports The Zimbabwean government has confirmed that its dramatic suspension of all raw mineral ore and lithium concentrate exports is a direct strike against sophisticated smuggling syndicates who have been robbing the nation blind by misdeclaring valuable multi-mineral [...]The post Caught red-handed — Government finally explains why it had to ban all raw mineral & lithium exports appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Ford Raptors, Masked Thugs, and a Reign of Terror: Professor Lovemore Madhuku Brutally Assaulted for Opposing ED2030
more.description: The brutal assault on Professor Lovemore Madhuku and members of his National Constitutional Assembly (NCA) at their Harare offices on March 1, 2026, represents a significant escalation in the crackdown on dissent in Zimbabwe. This report provides a comprehensive analysis of the incident, its political context, and the broader implications for democracy and the rule [...]The post Ford Raptors, Masked Thugs, and a Reign of Terror: Professor Lovemore Madhuku Brutally Assaulted for Opposing ED2030 first appeared on My Zimbabwe News.

more.title: ‘Multi-million cyber city dream alive’
more.description: Source: ‘Multi-million cyber city dream alive’ – The Standard Businessman and war veteran John Fadzisayi Jani says he remains confident that his proposed multi-billion-dollar cyber city project will proceed, despite an ongoing High Court dispute over Elmswood Farm, the land earmarked for the development. more.title: No eternal posts in Zanu PF: Mudenda
more.description: Source: No eternal posts in Zanu PF: Mudenda – The Standard Zanu PF secretary-general Jacob Mudenda reminded party members that no leadership position is eternal, including the presidency, and must never be treated as private property. He warned party members against personalising leadership positions, reminding them that offices outlive individuals. He was addressing a Mashonaland [...]The post No eternal posts in Zanu PF: Mudenda appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Buhera South MP violence case deferred
more.description: Source: Buhera South MP violence case deferred – The Standard The public violence case involving Buhera South Member of Parliament Ngonidzashe Mudekunye has been deferred to March 13 after the State applied for a postponement, citing an incomplete docket. Mudekunye appeared before Chimanimani magistrate Regis Mawarire last Tuesday. Prosecutor Hardlife Machando told the court that the matter was not ready for trial as [...]The post Buhera South MP violence case deferred appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation.

more.title: Missing Zanu PF councillor mystery rocks family

more.description: Source: Madhuku, NCA Members assaulted and injured at party office in Harare Zimbabwean opposition leader Lovemore Madhuku and several members of his party, the National Constitutional Assembly (NCA), were allegedly assaulted at the party’s offices in Harare on Sunday morning.
